title: (No AI) Meet the former Apple designer building a new AI interface at Hark

title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude Code and Cowork can control your computer
author: Jess Weatherbed
content:
![A screenshot of the warning message that appears in Claude before allowing the AI chatbot to control your computer.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/Anthropic-Claude-Code-Cowork-computer-control.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Claude will ask you for permission to autonomously perform taks on your computer. | Image by Anthropic

Anthropic has updated Claude to perform tasks in its Code and Cowork AI tools autonomously by using your computer for you. The new feature can be used to automatically open files, use web browsers and apps, and run dev tools "with no setup required," even when you're away from your computer, according to [Anthropic's announcement](https://claude.com/blog/dispatch-and-computer-use).

These new capabilities are available as a research preview for Claude Pro and Max subscribers, and computer usage is limited to macOS "for now," according to Anthropic. The feature [builds on autonomous capabilities](https://www.theverge.com/2024/10/22/24276822/anthopic-claude-3-5-sonnet-computer-use-ai) that were introduced on Claude's 3.5 Sonnet AI model in 2024, but now brings those to [the chatbot's C …](https://www.theverge.com/report/874308/anthropic-claude-code-opus-hype-moment)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/899430/anthropic-claude-code-cowork-ai-control-computer)

Anthropic has updated Claude to perform tasks in its Code and Cowork AI tools autonomously by using your computer for you. These new capabilities are available as a research preview for Claude Pro and Max subscribers, and computer usage is limited to macOS "for now," according to Anthropic. The feature builds on autonomous capabilities that were introduced on Claude's 3.5 Sonnet AI model in 2024, but now brings those to the chatbot's C …
Read the full story at The Verge.

title: (No AI) Google’s new Pixel 10 ads made me go ‘Wait, WHAT are they trying to sell?’
author: Sean Hollister
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/03/chrome_Apdz9O6Gdb.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Ever watch a TV ad and wonder, "How did _this_ get approved?" Today, Google has not one but two new ad spots for its six-month-old [Pixel 10 phones](https://www.theverge.com/reviews/772813/google-pixel-10-review), and… let's just say they may not come across as intended.

First, there's "With 100x Zoom," an ad that appears to suggest that if a vacation rental company lies to you about the view from your hotel room, _you should lie to all your friends and family, too!_

Take a watch below:

The [YouTube description](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=reNuCsQwqUE) makes Google's intent fairly clear: "So even if that breathtaking view you were promised turns out to be miles away, now you can zoom your way to a photo that makes it look like you were right there. …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/898992/google-pixel-10-ads-moving-on-stalker-lying-beach-view-100-zoom)

First, there's "With 100x Zoom," an ad that appears to suggest that if a vacation rental company lies to you about the view from your hotel room, you should lie to all your friends and family, too! 
Take a watch below:

The YouTube description makes Google's intent fairly clear: "So even if that breathtaking view you were promised turns out to be miles away, now you can zoom your way to a photo that makes it look like you were right there. …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Katie Drummond
title: (No AI) Chris Hayes Has Some Advice for Keeping Up With the News
dc:creator: Katie Drummond
content: The host of MS Now’s All In, knows how hard it is to stay current. But he also knows where you should focus your attention—and it starts with a sober view of AI.
contentSnippet: The host of MS Now’s All In, knows how hard it is to stay current. But he also knows where you should focus your attention—and it starts with a sober view of AI.

creator: Andrew Guthrie Ferguson
title: (No AI) Your Body Is Betraying Your Right to Privacy
dc:creator: Andrew Guthrie Ferguson
content: Attachment to smart devices and biometric surveillance leaves Americans more vulnerable to police searches than ever. Left unchecked it will only get worse.
contentSnippet: Attachment to smart devices and biometric surveillance leaves Americans more vulnerable to police searches than ever. Left unchecked it will only get worse.
